// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders verifies AC #4 of bead ddx-3bd7396a:
// a project config carrying agent.routing.profile_ladders causes ddx work
// --once --local to fail with a hard error naming the removed field.
func TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
  repository:
    url: "https://example.com/lib"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    profile_ladders:
      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
`), 0o644))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--json", "--once")
	require.Error(t, err, "execute-loop must fail when config has profile_ladders")
	// routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="asserts the rejection error names the retired field"
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "profile_ladders")
}

	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"

}

I’m simplifying the helper to a one-shot announcer with a synchronous fallback after `ExecuteContext` returns on cancellation. That removes the race while keeping the first-signal watcher in place, and I’m silencing Cobra usage output in the test blocker so the assertions only see the shutdown line.

    10	)
    11	
    12	const gracefulCancelMessage = "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully"
    13	
    14	// newGracefulCancelAnnouncer returns a one-shot notifier for root-level
    15	// cancellation. It prints the operator-visible shutdown line exactly once and
    16	// then calls stop so a second SIGINT/SIGTERM falls back to the default signal
    17	// disposition and can still kill a wedged cleanup path.
    18	func newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(out io.Writer, stop func()) func() {
    19		var once sync.Once
    20		return func() {
    21			once.Do(func() {
    22				_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, gracefulCancelMessage)
    23			})
    24			if stop != nil {
    25				stop()
    26			}
    27		}
    28	}
    29	
    30	// executeRootCommand runs the root command under the supplied context while a
    31	// background watcher translates cancellation into the graceful shutdown
    32	// message. Tests can call this with a cancellable context directly instead of
    33	// delivering real OS signals.
    34	func executeRootCommand(rootCmd *cobra.Command, ctx context.Context, stop func()) error {
    35		cancelAnnounce := newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(rootCmd.ErrOrStderr(), stop)
    36	
    37		done := make(chan struct{})
    38		go func() {
    39			select {
    40			case <-ctx.Done():
    41				cancelAnnounce()
    42			case <-done:
    43			}
    44		}()
    45		defer close(done)
    46	
    47		err := rootCmd.ExecuteContext(ctx)
    48		if ctx.Err() != nil {
    49			cancelAnnounce()
    50		}
    51		return err
    52	}
